Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The value '40 months' is explicitly stated in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1: 'The median follow-up was 40 months.'

Treatment Arm(s).value: ADT alone; ADT plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone (combination therapy)
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: "We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy)." Table 1 on page 5 also lists the two arms as "ADT Alone" and "Combination Therapy", with a footnote clarifying "Combination therapy was androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone." Therefore, the experimental arms are ADT alone and ADT plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 4, in the 'RESULTS' section under 'PATIENTS', it states: 'Between November 15, 2011, and January 17, 2014, a total of 1917 consenting patients at 111 U.K. and 5 Swiss sites underwent randomization (957 to ADT alone and 960 to combination therapy).' The 'combination therapy' is the experimental arm.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'A total of 1917 patients underwent randomization from November 2011 through January 2014.' This is further detailed on page 4, in the 'RESULTS' -> 'PATIENTS' section: 'a total of 1917 consenting patients at 111 U.K. and 5 Swiss sites underwent randomization (957 to ADT alone and 960 to combination therapy).' The total number of participants is the sum of patients in both arms: 957 + 960 = 1917.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'ABSTRACT' section states: 'We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy).' This describes the specific treatment protocol for the combination therapy arm.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: Abiraterone acetate (androgen biosynthesis inhibitor) and prednisolone (corticosteroid)
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states, 'We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy).' Page 2 describes abiraterone acetate as a 'selective, irreversible inhibitor of CYP17', which is an androgen biosynthesis inhibitor. Prednisolone is a corticosteroid.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Control Arm.evidence: In the 'METHODS' section on page 1, it states: 'We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy).' This indicates 'ADT alone' is the comparison group not receiving the experimental treatment. The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 also consistently refers to the 'ADT-alone group' as the comparison.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 2 states that 'quality of life' was a secondary outcome measure, but it explicitly mentions '(data not shown)', indicating that no specific scale or values for quality of life are reported in the document.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: On page 2, under the 'METHODS' section, the document states: 'Secondary outcome measures included adverse events, symptomatic skeletal events, progression-free survival (i.e., failure-free survival excluding biochemical failure), prostate cancer-specific survival, and (data not shown) quality of life.' This indicates that quality of life was a reported outcome.

Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 4, in the 'PATIENTS' section, it states: 'a total of 1917 consenting patients... underwent randomization (957 to ADT alone and 960 to combination therapy).' The 'ADT alone' group is the control arm. This is further confirmed in Table 1 on page 5 under 'ADT Alone (N=957)' and in Table 2 on page 10 under 'Intention-to-treat population Total no. of patients ADT Alone 957'.

Trial.value: THE SYSTEMIC THERAPY IN ADVANCING or Metastatic Prostate Cancer: Evaluation of Drug Efficacy (STAMPEDE) trial
Trial.evidence: On page 2, under the 'TRIAL DESIGN AND PATIENTS' section, the document explicitly states: 'THE SYSTEMIC THERAPY IN ADVANCING or Metastatic Prostate Cancer: Evaluation of Drug Efficacy (STAMPEDE) trial recruits patients...'. This is the full name of the clinical study referenced.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document reports median durations for subgroups of the combination group (23.7 months for patients whose duration was capped at 2 years and 33.2 months for patients who could continue through progression) in the 'TREATMENT' section on page 4, but a single overall median on-treatment duration for the entire treatment arm is not reported.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document states that ADT was 'given for at least 2 years' as a planned duration for the control arm in the 'PROCEDURES' section on page 3, but the median on-treatment duration for the control arm is not reported.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: Overall survival, defined as the time from randomization to death from any cause. The intermediate primary outcome was failure-free survival, defined as the time to the first of the following forms of treatment failure: biochemical (prostate-specific antigen [PSA]) failure; progression of local, lymph-node, or distant metastases; or death from prostate cancer.
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: The value is explicitly stated in the 'METHODS' section, under 'TRIAL DESIGN AND PATIENTS' on page 2: 'The primary outcome was overall survival, defined as the time from randomization to death from any cause. The intermediate primary outcome was failure-free survival, defined as the time to the first of the following forms of treatment failure: biochemical (prostate-specific antigen [PSA]) failure (see the protocol, available with the full text of this article at NEJM.org); progression of local, lymph-node, or distant metastases; or death from prostate cancer.'

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: Table 1 on page 5 categorizes patients by "Disease group" including "Newly diagnosed metastatic disease". The results are also reported by "Metastatic status" in Figure 1 (page 7) and Figure 2 (page 9), which includes patients with newly diagnosed metastatic disease, indicating reporting by synchronous metastases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: Table 1 on page 5 categorizes patients by "Disease group" including "Previously treated metastatic disease". The discussion on page 8 also mentions "men with relapsing disease and poor prognostic features", which refers to metachronous disease.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The document stratifies results by metastatic status (metastatic vs. nonmetastatic disease) but does not provide further subgroup analysis or reporting based on "high volume" or "high burden" of metastases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The document stratifies results by metastatic status (metastatic vs. nonmetastatic disease) but does not provide further subgroup analysis or reporting based on "low volume" of metastases.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: adverse events, symptomatic skeletal events, progression-free survival (i.e., failure-free survival excluding biochemical failure), prostate cancer-specific survival, and (data not shown) quality of life.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: The paper describes a comparison between 'ADT alone' (control arm) and 'ADT plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone' (treatment arm). Docetaxel was not part of the initial treatment regimen for either of these arms. While docetaxel is mentioned as a previous comparison within the broader STAMPEDE trial and as a second-line treatment after progression (Figure 3, page 11), the question specifically asks about administration 'before or during the trial' as part of the initial treatment arms being compared in this paper. This information is not reported for the specified treatment arm.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: The paper describes a comparison between 'ADT alone' (control arm) and 'ADT plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone' (treatment arm). Docetaxel was not part of the initial treatment regimen for either of these arms. While docetaxel is mentioned as a previous comparison within the broader STAMPEDE trial and as a second-line treatment after progression (Figure 3, page 11), the question specifically asks about administration 'before or during the trial' as part of the initial treatment arms being compared in this paper. This information is not reported for the specified control arm.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a primary report of a randomized controlled trial (RCT) on abiraterone for prostate cancer. It describes the trial design, methods, results, and adverse events. However, it does not contain an explicit 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence' for the RCT itself, which is typically an assessment performed by external reviewers (e.g., in a systematic review or meta-analysis) rather than being reported within the primary trial publication.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The 'METHODS' section in the abstract states: 'We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy).' This indicates that abiraterone acetate and prednisolone are the add-on treatments given in combination with ADT.

Treatment Class.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) alone and Combination therapy (ADT plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone)
Treatment Class.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy).' Additionally, Table 1 on page 5, footnote * clarifies: 'Combination therapy was androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone.'

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy). Local radiotherapy was also mandated for patients with node-negative, nonmetastatic disease and encouraged for those with positive nodes.
Type of Therapy.evidence: The 'ABSTRACT' section on page 1 states: 'We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy).' It also mentions: 'Local radiotherapy was mandated for patients with node-negative, nonmetastatic disease and encouraged for those with positive nodes.' The 'METHODS' section on page 2 further elaborates on ADT as 'orchiectomy or gonadotropin-releasing hormone [GnRH] agonists or antagonists'.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Found in Table 2, "Worst Adverse-Event Grade Reported during Entire Time in the Trial", under "Safety population", row "Grade 3-5", column "Combination Therapy". This is also confirmed in the "RESULTS" section on page 1: "Grade 3 to 5 adverse events occurred in 47% of the patients in the combination group".

Found in Table 2, "Worst Adverse-Event Grade Reported during Entire Time in the Trial", under "Safety population", row "Grade 3-5", column "ADT Alone". This is also confirmed in the "RESULTS" section on page 1: "and in 33% of the patients in the ADT-alone group".

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: The document reports "Grade 3 to 5 adverse events" (all-cause) but does not provide a separate count or percentage for *treatment-related* adverse events of grade 3 or higher in the treatment arm.
The document reports "Grade 3 to 5 adverse events" (all-cause) but does not provide a separate count or percentage for *treatment-related* adverse events of grade 3 or higher in the control arm.

Found in Table 2, "Worst Adverse-Event Grade Reported during Entire Time in the Trial", under "Safety population", row "Grade 5 only†", column "Combination Therapy". The footnote describes these events as occurring during the trial, implying they are treatment-related. This is also confirmed in the "RESULTS" section on page 1: "with nine grade 5 events" in the combination group.

Found in Table 2, "Worst Adverse-Event Grade Reported during Entire Time in the Trial", under "Safety population", row "Grade 5 only†", column "ADT Alone". The footnote describes these events as occurring during the trial, implying they are treatment-related. This is also confirmed in the "RESULTS" section on page 1: "with three grade 5 events" in the ADT-alone group.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The 'OVERALL SURVIVAL' section on page 4 states: 'There was strong evidence of a survival advantage in the combination group, with a 3-year survival of 83% as compared with 76% in the ADT-alone group (hazard ratio for death, 0.63; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.52 to 0.76; P<0.001) (Fig. 1A).'

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The 'OVERALL SURVIVAL' section on page 4 states: 'There was strong evidence of a survival advantage in the combination group, with a 3-year survival of 83% as compared with 76% in the ADT-alone group (hazard ratio for death, 0.63; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.52 to 0.76; P<0.001) (Fig. 1A).'

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Treatment.evidence: Figure 1C, 'Overall Survival in Patients with Metastatic Disease' on page 7, shows the Kaplan-Meier curve for combination therapy. Visually estimating the survival probability at 36 months (3 years) from the blue line yields approximately 72%.

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Control.evidence: Figure 1C, 'Overall Survival in Patients with Metastatic Disease' on page 7, shows the Kaplan-Meier curve for ADT alone. Visually estimating the survival probability at 36 months (3 years) from the black line yields approximately 60%.

OS Rate (%) | Metachronous | Treatment.evidence: The document provides subgroup analysis for 'metastatic disease' and 'nonmetastatic disease' at randomization (Figure 1C and 1E), which corresponds to synchronous status. It does not provide specific OS rates for patients with metachronous metastasis (metastasis after diagnosis) in the treatment arm.

OS Rate (%) | Metachronous | Control.evidence: The document provides subgroup analysis for 'metastatic disease' and 'nonmetastatic disease' at randomization (Figure 1C and 1E), which corresponds to synchronous status. It does not provide specific OS rates for patients with metachronous metastasis (metastasis after diagnosis) in the control arm.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: The document does not report OS rates specifically for patients with 'high volume' metastasis or mention 'CHAARTED criteria'.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Control.evidence: The document does not report OS rates specifically for patients with 'high volume' metastasis or mention 'CHAARTED criteria'.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The median overall survival for the overall treatment group is not explicitly reported as a numerical value in the text or tables. Figure 1A shows the Kaplan-Meier curve for overall survival in all patients, where the combination therapy curve does not reach the median (0.5 probability) within the 54-month follow-up period, indicating it is 'not reached' or greater than 54 months, but no exact value is provided.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: The median overall survival for the overall control group is not explicitly reported as a numerical value in the text or tables. Figure 1A shows the Kaplan-Meier curve for overall survival in all patients, where the ADT alone curve crosses the median (0.5 probability) between 42 and 48 months, but an exact numerical value is not provided in the document.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: The document does not explicitly report median overall survival for patients with 'high volume metastasis by CHAARTED criteria' in the experimental arm. While Figure 1C presents overall survival for 'Patients with Metastatic Disease', it does not specify 'high volume by CHAARTED criteria' nor does it provide an explicit median OS value in the text or tables.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: The document does not explicitly report median overall survival for patients with 'high volume metastasis by CHAARTED criteria' in the control arm. While Figure 1C presents overall survival for 'Patients with Metastatic Disease', it does not specify 'high volume by CHAARTED criteria' nor does it provide an explicit median OS value in the text or tables.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: The document does not explicitly report median overall survival for patients with 'low volume metastasis by CHAARTED criteria' in the experimental arm. While Figure 1E presents overall survival for 'Patients with Nonmetastatic Disease', it does not specify 'low volume by CHAARTED criteria' nor does it provide an explicit median OS value in the text or tables (the curve does not reach the median within 54 months).

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: The document does not explicitly report median overall survival for patients with 'low volume metastasis by CHAARTED criteria' in the control arm. While Figure 1E presents overall survival for 'Patients with Nonmetastatic Disease', it does not specify 'low volume by CHAARTED criteria' nor does it provide an explicit median OS value in the text or tables (the curve does not reach the median within 54 months).

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in the 'FAILURE-FREE SURVIVAL' section on page 6: 'restricted mean failure-free survival time: 43.9 months in the combination group'.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in the 'FAILURE-FREE SURVIVAL' section on page 6: 'restricted mean failure-free survival time: 30.0 months in the ADT-alone group'.
